tools.name: list_leagues
tools.description: List every league/data group this API serves (nfl, nba, mlb, nhl, pga, dfs, kalshi, polymarket, reference) with table counts and availability. No credentials needed. Start here, then call list_tables.

tools.name: list_tables
tools.description: List every queryable table in one league with a one-line summary each. No credentials needed. Follow with describe_table before querying.

tools.name: describe_table
tools.description: Full contract for one table: fields with types and descriptions, the filters query_table accepts (with operators like __gte/__lte/__between/__like and required filter combinations), page-size limits, primary key, and a runnable example URL. No credentials needed. Always call this before query_table

tools.name: query_table
tools.description: Query any table. Returns `{"<table>": [rows], "limit": N, "next_from_id": M|null}`; page by re-calling with from_id set to the previous next_from_id until it is null. filters is a flat object of `field` or `field__op` keys from describe_table (e.g. {"season_id": 2024, "day__between": "20250101,20250

tools.name: get_record
tools.description: Fetch a single row by primary key. Returns the row object. Requires an API key; costs 1 record of quota.

tools.name: search_players
tools.description: Fuzzy player-name search (typo-tolerant). Currently NBA only — for other leagues query the players table with a name filter via query_table. Returns player rows ordered by match quality. Requires an API key; rows count against quota.

tools.name: games_on_date
tools.description: Games scheduled/played on one calendar date (US-Eastern) for nba, mlb, or nhl; defaults to today. Returns the same envelope as query_table on the games table. NFL is week-based, not date-based — for nfl, use query_table on nfl/games with season_id + week filters instead. Requires an API key; rows co

tools.name: game_markets
tools.description: Prediction-market contracts attached to one sporting event: Kalshi events+markets and/or Polymarket events+markets linked via (league_code, competition_id). game_id is the game's id from the league's games table (golf: the tournament id). Returns `{"kalshi": {events, markets}, "polymarket": {events,

tools.name: graphql_query
tools.description: Run a GraphQL query against the same data with relations — nest from a game to its teams/players/odds in one call. Root fields are leagues (nfl, nba, …), each exposing its tables with the same filter arguments as REST plus `first`/`after` pagination. Do NOT send introspection queries (the schema is

tools.name: api_usage
tools.description: Your key's quota position: plan, monthly record limit, records used, remaining, and when the quota resets. Requires an API key; costs 1 record.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "list_leagues" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.
